original_hypothesis.title: recover() Lacks Whitelist Check Allowing Owner to Mint Unbacked asdUSDC from Arbitrary Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ASDUSDC.recover() does not require _usdcVersion to be whitelisted. The owner can call recover() with any ERC20 token address (including worthless tokens airdropped to the contract). This mints asdUSDC proportional to the non-whitelisted token's balance using its decimals() for scaling. The owner receives unbacked asdUSDC that can be exchanged for real whitelisted USDC via withdraw(), draining legitimate depositors' funds.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The deposit and withdraw functions both check the whitelist, but recover() — which also mints asdUSDC — does not. This inconsistency allows minting backed by non-USDC tokens.

proof_of_signal.pattern: No require(whitelistedUSDCVersions[_usdcVersion]) in recover(); mints asdUSDC from any token balance

preconditions: Owner (or compromised owner key) calls recover with non-whitelisted token
Non-whitelisted token has non-zero balance in the contract (via airdrop or direct transfer)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: recover() creates asdUSDC backed by arbitrary tokens; deposit/withdraw enforce whitelist but recover does not
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Owner-only function — requires compromised or malicious owner
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time non-whitelisted tokens exist in contract

operational_questions: Is the owner a multisig or EOA?
Could airdropped tokens create significant recover() attack surface?
validation_requirements: Call recover with non-whitelisted token address
Verify asdUSDC minted without whitelist check

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Send worthless ERC20 token to asdUSDC contract
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Receive asdUSDC minted based on worthless token balance
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Withdraw real whitelisted USDC using the minted asdUSDC

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: asdUSDC becomes under-collateralized for real USDC

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: recover() calls with non-whitelisted token addresses
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: asdUSDC supply increase without corresponding USDC deposits
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Owner minting followed by immediate withdrawals

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
